rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Prevailing Party And Fixing Attorneys Fees Recoverable As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEY'S FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is DENIED. The post-judgment motion decided by this Court and subject to the latest appellate decision, is not yet resolved as this Court was instructed to "proceed to decide whether factual and legal issues that may need to be decided in order to determine whether the bar on Epstein's ownership of the property should remain in place." The Court has not yet been presented with argument or evidence on this point. If and when this issue is resolved, upon presentation of argument and evidence, the determination of the prevailing party may be revisited. As to the determination of the prevailing party on appeal from the Court's prior decision, the Court finds that there was no prevailing party that obtained a clear and unqualified win. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition To Modify Or Vacate Final Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2024 line 2. PETITIONER REGINALD HINDLEY'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON AMENDED PETITION TO MODIFY OR VACATE FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD is GRANTED. Pursuant to the Court of Appeal decision in action CPF-14-513720 the Court, not the arbitrator, was to determine the scope of the injunction and is to determine whether the injunction should be lifted. In light of this decision, the Court shall vacate the arbitration award, even though both the trial Court and the Court of Appeals confirmed that, at least on the issue of the scope of the injunction, the arbitrator was correct. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). RESPONDENTS MARK EPSTEIN AND SAMETC, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEY'S FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS ($42,655) is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: JAMESTOWN SA LLC; ET AL VS. JONATHAN DISHOTSKY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANTS RICHARD KALUZA, ERIN THOMPSON AND COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the paragraphs regarding tort of another. In accordance with the Court's prior ruling, Plaintiff resolved the issue of uncertainty and it is now clear that the tort of another is pled singling out one of the multiple wrongdoers in this action. This is not a proper application of the tort of another doctrine. There are no allegations in the operative complaint that plaintiffs named non-moving co-defendants as defendants in this action as a result of the moving co-defendants negligence, as opposed to the non-moving co-defendants own negligence. The motion is denied as to the request for emotional distress damages in conjunction with the fraud and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that recovery of such damages are not available for intentional torts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2024 line 4. REFEREE AMY HARRINGTON'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF FINAL REPORT OF SALE OF REAL PROPERTY AND FOR APPROVAL OF REFEREE FEES AND COSTS Hearing Required on the approval of sale. Plaintiff's request to charge the Partition Referee with the responsibility to initially review the apportionment of attorney's fees and make a recommendation to the Court made in April 10, 2024 Declaration of Robert Weiss, is granted. Plaintiff to bear the cost of this unilaterally made request via a declaration 15 calendar days before the hearing. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT Q HOANG VS. ANDERSEN, JUNG & CO., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT SAM KWONG'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Court previously sustained the demurrer with leave to amend to address all issues raised by the demurrer. The Plaintiff clarified the capacity in which the defendant is being sued (addressing the certificate of merit issue) and failed to allege any facts supporting a 3 -year statute of limitations (e.g. injury to real property) or facts supporting breach and damages caused by the moving party's breach. Opposition to the demurrer fails to suggest any possible amendments to cure these issues. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
